description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with a reddish-brown color and several visible roof tiles, some of which appear slightly raised or missing. The upper story is clad in light gray panels with dark brown timber framing, evoking a traditional half-timbered architectural style. The lower portion of the house is constructed from a blue-gray stone or concrete material, with a few small, irregularly shaped patches suggesting weathering or repairs. The structure is elevated on four legs or stilts, with the front and rear legs appearing straight and the side legs slightly angled. There are no visible windows or doors, giving the house a simple, utilitarian appearance. The overall design suggests a compact, two-story residential building suitable for a dense urban or village setting.
description: A compact, two-story house with a steep reddish-brown gable roof, half-timbered upper walls, and a blue-gray stone base on stilts. The exterior is simple, with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_0: Front view shows the triangular gable end, half-timbered upper wall, and blue-gray stone base, with no visible entry or windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the long, sloped roof, the timber-framed upper story, and the blue-gray base, with angled support legs.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, showing the gable end and half-timbered wall, but the base appears darker and more shadowed.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, with the same roof and wall features, but the base appears slightly more irregular.
attribute_entryway_design: No visible entryway; simple facade with no steps, porch, or decorative elements
